c. L. Graves, chief deputy in the
county engineers office, has an
nounced his candidacy for the Of
flee.


Judge Dunn, at Occur d'AIene, has
entered an order permitting the
Washington Water Power Co. to re-
tain it's bear trap dam at Post Falls
until the case is decided in the SUI
preme court of Idaho. This means
that he water power of the city of
Spokane will not be interfered with
during the coming summer.


r. E. Strahorn's offer to pay
$352,O0 for the city hall property in
case of receiving franchise for
the North Coast railway has been
ordered spread of record by the city
council


Monday morning at 10 will be
held the funeral services of Win
field S. Gibson, the old soldier
whose dead body was found near
the foothills recently. The services
will be at the Turnbull chapel, in
charge of Reno post, G. A. r. Bur
ial at Orchard prairie.


Earl G. Constantine, teacher at
the South Central high school, has
accepted a place with the Trustee
Co. Constantine was an active fig
ure in the campaign for the $1,000
900 issue of park bonds.


Nine delegates from the Spokane
Ad Mems club will go to San Fran
cisco next week to boost for Spo-
kane as the place of meeting for the
Pacific Coast AID Mems association
in 1911. A rousing meeting of the
Spokane club was held last night, at


which 1t was voted that the dele
cation proceed to Frisco with full
complement of booster dope for the
Inland Empire. r. e. Bigelow of
| Spokane is president of the coast
association.
Joseph o. Hollis, age ~, died
from the shock following an opera
ton at the Sacred Heart hospital
Yesterday afternoon. He is sur
vived by two sons and there daugh.
tiers.


A hot fight is pending in the city


council over the operation on the
streets of news carts by newsboys
John w. Graham & Co. have remon
strated against permitting the carts


to run.


Managers of the Wilson campaign
deny that they have planned tc
commit the republican county con
| vention to a west side man for
United States senator. D. T. Han


says that the administration may


be Endorsed, but that he does not
know of the move against Poindex
ter. However, the Poindexter mer


have been aroused and will fight


the proposed resolution to a finish
| if it is introduced.


The ElIers piano house Is moving
| to 416 Sprague avenue, which it will
occupy during the progress of worl
on its new building at Post and
| Sprague. A. II. Eilers is here from
Portland, completing the "details
for the commencement of work OF
the new building.


Jacob Schiller, at the meeting o.


the city council last night, denied
that he had connected the purchase
of a new automobile by Councilman
Lambert with the passage of the
railway Franchise. 1t was charged


that Schiller had made the state
ment at meeting of the Peoples
Terminal Rate committee on Thurs
